mnemonics.title: BMW Categories Quick Recall
mnemonics.content: YELLOW: Anatomy, Soiled, Medicines (A-S-M) = 'Yam So Med' | RED: Reuse-contaminated plastic items | WHITE: Sharp Wounds from needles | BLUE: Broken glass, metal implants

mnemonics.title: BMW Hazards Memory Trick
mnemonics.content: I-SPOT: Infectious, Sharps, Pharmaceutical, Organic (pathological), Toxic/Chemical, Radioactive - remembering 'I Shar P Or To R'

mnemonics.title: Waste Hierarchy
mnemonics.content: R-R-R-T-D: Reduce → Reuse → Recycle → Treat → Dispose - Think 'Reduce Reuse Recycle to Dispose'

acronyms.usage: General term for medical/healthcare waste requiring special management
International body providing guidelines for biomedical waste management
Indian regulatory body that authorizes healthcare facilities for BMW handling

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a traffic signal: Yellow (warning/human) → Red (danger/contaminated) → White (pure but dangerous/sharp) → Blue (cold/metal-glass)
Picture human body: Yellow = internal organs; Red = blood vessels; White = bones (sharp); Blue = prosthetics/metals

analogies.analogy: Like sorting laundry: whites (sharps), reds (blood-contaminated), darks (pathological), delicates (chemicals) - each needs different handling
Like a ticking time bomb - contains dormant pathogens that can 'explode' into disease if not properly deactivated (treated)
Like stepping on a nail - the puncture creates a direct highway for pathogens into your bloodstream
